name: Jálama from Puerto de San Martin
name_original: Jálama desde Puerto de San Martin
region_intro: Hiking around Eljas offers a variety of trails through mountainous terrain and historical paths. The region is characterized by its peaks, such as Jálama, and features ancient routes like the Calzada Romana. Hikers can expect a mix of ascents to viewpoints and more gentle circular walks through the landscape. Best hiking trails around Eljas The most popular hiking route is San Martín de Trevejo to Puerto de San Martín via Calzada Romana, a 7.8 miles (12.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 54 minutes to complete. This moderate route features historical Roman road sections and mountain pass views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Jálama from Puerto de San Martin, a moderate 6.4 miles (10.2 km) path. This trail ascends to the Jálama peak, offering panoramic views from the mountain. Local hikers also love the Ermita del Espéritu Santa from Valverde del Fresno — circular the Camino del Espírita Santo, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail leading through varied local scenery, often completed in about 1 hour 48 minutes. Hiking around Eljas is defined by mountain peaks, historical paths, and circular routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Eljas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 130 hikers have used komoot to explore Eljas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Eljas?
A: There are over 10 hiking trails around Eljas listed on komoot, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 5 easy routes, 6 moderate, and 2 more challenging options, ensuring there's something for every hiker.

Q: Are there easy or beginner-friendly hiking routes in Eljas?
A: Yes, Eljas offers several easy routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1273740212/ermita-del-esperitu-santa-from-valverde-del-fresno-circular-the-camino-del-espirita-santo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Espéritu Santa from Valverde del Fresno — circular the Camino del Espírita Santo&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.1-mile (6.6 km) circular trail.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in Eljas?
A: The best times for hiking in Eljas are typically spring and autumn, when temperatures are mild and the landscape is vibrant. Summer can be hot, especially at midday, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. Winter offers a different charm, though some higher elevation trails might require appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any circular walks available around Eljas?
A: Yes, Eljas features several appealing circular walks. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1273740212/ermita-del-esperitu-santa-from-valverde-del-fresno-circular-the-camino-del-espirita-santo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Espéritu Santa from Valverde del Fresno — circular the Camino del Espírita Santo&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy route. For a moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1272046077/jalama-circular-from-dsa-390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jálama — circular from DSA-390&lt;/a&gt;, offering mountain views.

Q: What kind of attractions can I see along the hiking trails in Eljas?
A: Hiking trails in Eljas often lead to historical sites and natural landmarks. You can explore ancient routes like the Calzada Romana, or visit settlements such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6050936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín de Trevejo&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features religious buildings like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3555793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Holy Spirit&lt;/a&gt; and offers panoramic views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4958182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Jálama Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Eljas suitable for families with children?
A: Many of the easier and moderate trails around Eljas are suitable for families, especially those looking for shorter distances and less elevation gain. The circular routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1273740212/ermita-del-esperitu-santa-from-valverde-del-fresno-circular-the-camino-del-espirita-santo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Espéritu Santa from Valverde del Fresno&lt;/a&gt;, are often good choices for a family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Eljas?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in the Eljas region. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to check for any specific local regulations or protected areas that might have restrictions.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Eljas?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from historical Roman roads to challenging mountain ascents, and the rewarding panoramic views from peaks like Jálama.

Q: Are there any hidden gem hikes or less-known trails in Eljas?
A: While popular routes like those to Jálama are well-loved, exploring the network of trails can reveal quieter paths. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1272046077/jalama-circular-from-dsa-390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jálama — circular from DSA-390&lt;/a&gt; for a moderate loop that offers stunning views away from the most frequented paths.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Eljas?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. In general, you can find parking in or near the villages that serve as starting points for many routes, such as San Martín de Trevejo or Valverde del Fresno. For more remote trailheads, roadside parking might be available, but it's always best to plan ahead.

Q: Are there any permits required for hiking in Eljas?
A: No specific permits are generally required for hiking on the public trails around Eljas. However, always respect private property, follow marked paths, and adhere to any local signage regarding access or environmental protection.
tips:
At 1,487 metres above sea level, it is one of the highest peaks in the Sierra de Gata.
The route to climb Pico Jálama is popular with hikers and offers spectacular views of the region. The route begins in the town of Acebo and passes by the Mirador de La Ventosa and Puerto de Perales.
